title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Vincent Easton waits anxiously under the big clock at Victoria Station, fearing that Theodora Darrel will not fulfill her promise to run away with him. He reflects on their meeting two weeks prior at Haidme Manor, where he first kissed her under a plum tree. Despite his status as an ordinary citrus grower from the Transvaal province, he had asked her to leave her luxurious London life to be with him. Although he worries about the potential scandal and doubts her commitment, Theodora arrives at the station dressed in black.

After hiring a porter, the pair boards a train and travels through southern London. During the journey, Vincent contemplates Theodora's mysterious nature; while she is the wife of the popular and charming Richard Darrel, Vincent knows almost nothing about her true thoughts or her marriage. He feels a sense of fear regarding her elusive aura, yet he seeks comfort through physical affection. When he asks if she will regret their decision, Theodora denies it, admitting only that she is afraid of the feeling of happiness.

The couple arrives in Dover around 5 pm to stay overnight before crossing the channel. While embracing in their hotel room, they are interrupted by a knock at the door. A male tea server enters, places tea on a coffee table near Theodora, and departs after checking if they require further assistance.
